build_system: Use Gradle 8.11+ with the included wrapper (`./gradlew`). The root `build.gradle` configures Java 21 toolchain for all subprojects, applies Spotless (Google Java Format 1.35.0) and JaCoCo 0.8.14 to every submodule, and wires `jacocoTestReport` to run automatically after each `test` task. Run `./gradlew build` for a full build including tests and format checks; run `./gradlew spotlessApply` before every commit and push to apply formatting and inject Apache 2.0 license headers — omitting this step will cause CI failures. The project publishes via `com.vanniktech.maven.publish` to Maven Central (Sonatype) using in-memory GPG signing driven by `GPG_PRIVATE_KEY` and `GPG_PASSWORD` environment variables; use `./scripts/release.sh` to cut releases.
test_infrastructure: Tests use JUnit 5 with AssertJ assertions; run per-module with `./gradlew :<module>:test` or all at once via `./gradlew build`. Integration correctness is validated in `reggie-integration-tests` through `CorrectnessTest.java` against PCRE and RE2 suites (currently 91.3% PCRE pass rate). JaCoCo aggregate coverage is generated with `./gradlew jacocoAggregateReport` (output at `build/reports/jacoco/aggregate/html/index.html`) and enforced via `./gradlew jacocoVerify` with a 70% overall line-coverage minimum; reggie-codegen targets 75% line/70% branch, reggie-runtime 75% line, reggie-processor 65% line. Known-failure tests are skipped by default and enabled with `-Dreggie.test.knownFailures=true`. Performance is measured with 322 JMH benchmarks in `reggie-benchmark`; run `./gradlew :reggie-benchmark:benchmarkAndReport` and save a baseline with `./gradlew :reggie-benchmark:saveBaseline`.
sharp_edges: Always run `./gradlew spotlessApply` before every commit and push; `./gradlew build` runs `spotlessCheck` and will fail if formatting or license headers are missing. Bytecode generation changes must be applied to both `RuntimeCompiler.java` (runtime path) and `ReggieMatcherBytecodeGenerator.java` (compile-time path) — updating only one silently breaks the other path. Never hardcode local variable slots in bytecode generators; always compute them as `highestSlot + 1` to avoid `VerifyError`. Use `BytecodeUtil.pushInt(mv, value)` instead of `mv.visitLdcInsn(int)` to prevent accidental autoboxing. Every `PatternInfo` subclass `structuralHashCode()` must include all fields that affect generated bytecode, or cache collisions will produce wrong match results. The `@RegexPattern` annotation processor throws `UnsupportedOperationException` at build time for patterns that would trigger known engine bugs; use `Reggie.compile()` (runtime path) for those patterns, which transparently falls back to `java.util.regex`. Temporary documents must be stored in `doc/temp/` (gitignored) and must never be committed.
pr_ci_rules: CI runs on every push and pull-request targeting `main` via `.github/workflows/ci.yml`, using a single Ubuntu-latest job with Java 21 (Temurin) and the Gradle Actions cache. The only required CI step is `./gradlew build`, which encompasses compilation, Spotless format checks, all unit and integration tests, and JaCoCo report generation; test results are uploaded as artifacts with a 14-day retention. All GitHub Actions are pinned to full commit SHAs. PR checklist requires: all existing tests pass, new tests added, documentation updated if applicable, and commits signed. Performance benchmark runs are manual-trigger only via the separate CI workflow.
file_layout: The repository is a six-module Gradle project: `reggie-annotations` (single `@RegexPattern` annotation), `reggie-codegen` (core pipeline: `ast/`, `parsing/`, `automaton/`, `analysis/`, `codegen/` sub-packages), `reggie-processor` (annotation processor for compile-time path), `reggie-runtime` (public API in `Reggie.java`, `RuntimeCompiler.java`, `ReggieMatcher.java`), `reggie-benchmark` (322 JMH benchmarks), and `reggie-integration-tests` (PCRE/RE2 correctness tests). Unit tests live under `src/test/java/` in each module mirroring the production package structure; integration tests are in `reggie-integration-tests/src/test/java/`. Permanent documentation goes in `doc/` (`ARCHITECTURE.md`, `TUTORIAL-RUNTIME.md`, `TUTORIAL-COMPILE-TIME.md`, `plans/`); temporary working documents must go in `doc/temp/` (gitignored). `AGENTS.md` is the single source of truth for development guidance; `CLAUDE.md` is a generated redirect that must never be edited directly.
